text: Adapting large language models (LLMs) to specialized downstream tasks relies heavily on efficient fine-tuning techniques. However, implementing these methods across diverse model architectures often demands significant engineering effort. To address this, we introduce LLaMA-Factory, a unified framework that scales and simplifies the fine-tuning process by integrating a wide array of cutting-edge efficiency-driven algorithms. Through its intuitive web interface, LLaMA-Board, the framework offers a no-code solution for customizing over 100 distinct LLMs. Our empirical evaluations demonstrate that LLaMA-Factory maintains high performance in language modeling and text generation while significantly reducing computational overhead. Since its release, the project has gained substantial community traction, garnering over 25,000 stars and 3,000 forks on GitHub (https://github.com/hiyouga/LLaMA-Factory).
